text: Energy Pedals One provides an introduction for the robotic energy devices, as well as providing an orientation for the foot pedals and their controls.
The robotic system's right hand manipulates scissors capable of performing monopolar cutting and coagulation.
And you'll see that the screen provides instruction as to which energy modality to apply and when to apply it.
The robotic system's left arm is equipped with a grasper designed for bipolar cautery, visible in the surgical field.
The robotic arm grasps the center of a barbell-like object, applying bipolar coagulation to the targeted area.
Also take note of the interface on your screen, which will provide you feedback as to where your foot is in relation to the pedal you want to press.
The interface on the screen visually highlights the change from the left arm to the right arm, indicating the surgeon's right foot is now hovering over the right arm foot pedals.

And though the objects appear at random and there's no set sequence, a quick tip is that the small spheres will always be for monopolar cut, the larger spheres will always be for monopolar coag, and the barbell-like objects will always be for bipolar coag.
